data.title: Oil futures slide as U.S. crude stockpiles rose more than expected
data.description:
Crude oil futures snapped a two-day positive streak on Wednesday as a bigger than expected build in U.S. crude inventories sparked demand concerns, pushing Middle East tensions to the back burner.

Commercial crude oil stockpiles excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 5.5M barrels to 426M barrels in the week ended October 18 and were ~4% below the five-year average for the time of year, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal had predicted crude stockpiles would rise by 800K barrels.

U.S. refineries ran at 89.5% of capacity, up 1.8 percentage points from the previous week, compared to a small decline forecast in the WSJ survey, yielding a build in gasoline stocks of 878K barrels to 213.6M barrels, ~3% below the five-year average, compared to expectations of a 1.3M-barrel decline.

The report added to persistent concerns over China's demand outlook and prospects of a global supply surplus next year that sent oil futures falling more than 7% last week.

Also pressuring oil prices on Wednesday, the dollar index rose to its highest since late July; a firmer dollar can hurt demand for dollar-denominated oil from buyers using other currencies.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for December delivery closed -1.3% to $70.77/bbl, while front-month December Brent (CO1:COM) ended -1.4% to $74.96/bbl.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) rose ahead of the weekly domestic inventory report that is expected to show a contraction in the surplus to 143B cf from 163B cf the previous week; Nymex front-month settled +1.3% at $2.342/MMBtu.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

The third-warmest October in the U.S. in more than 60 years and warm forecasts for early November have significantly weighed on natural gas prices, with Nymex gas down 21% month-to-date, a move Goldman Sachs analysts say is appropriately sized.

However, Goldman says it maintains its $3.10/MMBtu Henry Hub forecast for now, "given that the potential weather-driven swings in demand over the coming weeks and months are significantly larger than what we observed thus far in October."

data.title: Gold rally reflects growth of dollar alternatives in warning to West, El-Erian says
data.description:
Gold prices slid more than 1% Wednesday after initially hitting yet another record high, as a stronger dollar and higher U.S. Treasury yields likely prompted some amount of profit-taking.

Following sustained support from safe-haven demand tied to the Middle East conflict and the upcoming U.S. presidential election, SP Angel analysts said the BRICS summit is also in the spotlight, with de-dollarization talks underway set to boost gold further.

Money managers have raised their gold bullish positions this week, with short-only positions falling, and exchange-traded fund holdings also continue to grow, hovering ~84M oz, SP Angel added.

On Monday and Tuesday, SPDR Gold Trust (NYSEARCA:GLD), the largest physical gold ETF, attracted ~$580M of net positive inflows, according to Samer Hasn of XS.com.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed -1.1% to $2714.40/oz, after setting a fresh record of $2,772.60/oz earlier in the session, and front-month October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled -3.4% to $33.640/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Western countries should take surging gold prices more seriously, former Pimco CEO Mohamed El-Erian said this week, as the metal's move reflects increasing interest in alternatives to the dollar-based financial system.

"Something strange has happened to the price of gold over the past year," El-Erian wrote in the Financial Times. "In setting one record level after the other, it seems to have decoupled from its traditional historical influencers, such as interest rates, inflation and the dollar."

"What is at stake here is not just the erosion of the dollar's dominant role but also a gradual change in the operation of the global system," according to El-Erian.

"No other currency or payment system is able and willing to displace the dollar at the core of the system and there is a practical limit to reserve diversification," he said. "But an increasing number of little pipes are being built to go around this core; and a growing number of countries are interested and increasingly involved."

data.title: Tesla rips 12% gain as investors eye the plan for affordable EVs
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) rallied late on Wednesday after the Austin-based company impressed investors on the margin line. The automotive ex-credits gross margin rate was reported at 17.1% to easily clear the consensus estimate of 15.1%. Operating margin was reported at 10.8% of sales to improve from last quarter's mark of 6.3%, and top last year's mark of 7.6%.

On the earnings conference call, CEO Elon Musk took a victory lap over what he said was a record quarter. He noted that Tesla (TSLA) just produced its 7th million vehicle. Musk said more affordable models will be produced by Tesla (TSLA) in the first half of 2025. He expects vehicle production to increase by 20% to 30% in 2025. Cybercab production is anticipated to be scaled up aggressively in 2026. Regarding autonomy, Musk said miles-per-intervention metrics continue to improve, and the company thinks FSD will exceed human-level driving safety by Q2 of 2025. Ride-hailing is expected to be live in Texas and California next year.

Musk said the future of Tesla (TSLA) is autonomous when answering a question if a mass-market $25K vehicle would be introduced. The implication is that the Model 2 will not resemble a typical car and may not have a steering wheel. The Tesla Semi was noted to be preferred by the PepsiCo (PEP) drivers taking part of the all-electric semi initiative.

On Wall Street, CFRA analyst Garrett Nelson reminded investors that expectations were low heading into the release after four consecutive bottom-line misses and a Robotaxi Day event that left investors with more questions than answers. Nelson said the key question is the sustainability of TSLA's Q3 gross margin. He also thinks consensus estimates that assume nearly 80% EPS growth between 2024 and 2026 are unrealistically high. Wedbush Securities was more positive as usual. Analyst Dan Ives said with price cuts fully in the rearview mirror now, Tesla (TSLA) showed off its ability to expand its margins as the company continues its AI/FSD transformation over the coming years.

On Seeking Alpha, Victor Dergunov, Investing Group Leader for The Financial Prophet:The most significant takeaway is that Tesla's profitability improved more than anticipated, which was a primary concern going into the report. Moreover, the substantial EPS outperformance implies that Tesla could continue improving profitability more than expected as we advance

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) were up 11.55% a 6:14 p.m.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile secures initial contract with Space Development Agency
data.description:
• AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ:ASTS) on Wednesday announced that it has been selected by the Space Development Agency for an agreement to compete for upcoming prototype demonstration projects under the Hybrid Acquisition for proliferated Low-earth Orbit program.
  • ASTS +6.09% after hours to $27.86.
  • The HALO program is an initiative designed to accelerate the development and deployment of advanced space technologies.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Auto parts stocks slump as O'Reilly lowers outlook on 'soft demand environment'
data.description:
Shares of O’Reilly Automotive (NASDAQ:ORLY) turned defensive in after-hours trading on Wednesday after the company lowered its profit and sales outlook for the year to reflect disappointing results in the recently reported quarter.

“Our comparable store sales increased 1.5% in the third quarter, as we faced broad-based consumer pressures and a soft demand environment on both the professional and DIY sides of our business,” O’Reilly CEO Brad Beckham said as comparable stores sales were down significantly from +8.7% in the same quarter last year.

For FY24, comparable store sales are now expected to increase by 2% to 3% from initial guidance of up 2%-4% while total revenue is revised lower to $16.6B to $16.8B from previous estimates of $16.7B to $16.9B. The updated sales outlook is just slightly above the consensus estimate of $16.75B.

Earnings per share have also been revised lower, now expected to be between $40.60 to $41.10 per share from the company’s initial projection of $40.75 to $41.25 per share, and below the estimated $41.15.

For the current Q3, the auto parts retailer earned a profit of $11.41 per share, an increase of 6% year-over-year, but 13 cents short of expectations. Total revenue improved 4% to $4.36B but was also less than expected.

The company’s lowered outlook is weighing on similar names in the sector with Advance Auto Parts (AAP) down 0.7%, AutoZone (AZO) down 2%, and Genuine Parks Company (GPC), down 0.5% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands in charts: Casino revenues dips sequentially in Q3
Seres cut to underweight by JP Morgan on Vowst sale
data.description:
• JP Morgan has downgraded Seres Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MCRB) to underweight, citing Nestle’s acquisition of the microbiome treatment Vowst, which was developed by Seres (MCRB).
  • The investment bank said the sale takes near-term pressure off the company by extending its cash runway into Q4 2025 and removing debt overhang.
  • However, the bank also said the sale makes it difficult to find a near-term fundamental growth driver to help improve sentiment in the shares.
  • “Overall, JPMC expects shares to be a relative underperformer in its coverage as it thinks the pipeline at Seres (MCRB) remains a ‘show-me’ story and expects MCRB to underperform relative to the rest of its broader coverage,” the bank added.
  • Seres (MCRB) completed the sale of its Vowst business to Nestle in late September.

data.title: Pfizer, Merck pneumonia shots gain ACIP endorsement for wider indication
data.description:
• The CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices has voted overwhelmingly to endorse lowering the age to receive either Pfizer's (NYSE:PFE) or Merck's (NYSE:MRK) pneumococcal disease vaccine to 50 and older.
  • Pfizer markets Prevnar 20, while Merck sells Capvaxive. The committee vote was 14-1 in favor.
  • Merck's vaccine has advantages over Pfizer's. Merck has said that Capvaxive covers the serotypes responsible for approximately 84% of invasive pneumococcal disease (IPD) cases in adults 50 and older compared to ~52% covered by Prevnar 20. For adults 65 and older, the figures are 85% and 51%.
  • For the expanded coverage to take place, the ACIP endorsement needs approval for CDC Director Many Cohen.
